disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No haematemesis, no coffee-ground vomit, no melaena, no fainting on standing (no acute UGIB)
trigger_question_examples: Have you vomited blood or anything like coffee-grounds, or passed black tarry stools?
Do you feel faint or nearly black out when you stand?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No blood or coffee-ground vomit, no black stools, and I don't faint standing up.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Chest/epigastric tightness is non-exertional and non-radiating, at rest after the trigger, with hyperventilation
trigger_question_examples: Is the chest tightness brought on by exertion or spreading to your arm/jaw?
Did it start at rest right after reading the results?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started at rest after reading it, doesn't spread to my arm or jaw, and I'm breathing fast.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Symptoms began immediately after reading results; prior health-anxiety episodes; hyperventilating
trigger_question_examples: When did this start relative to reading the results?
Does it feel like previous anxiety/panic episodes?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It started the moment I read the results and it feels like my past health-anxiety episodes.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Am I bleeding internally? Do I need an ambulance?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Screen UGIB and cardiac red flags first; if negative and the picture is classic panic, calmly de-escalate with paced breathing and an explicit UGIB/ACS safety-net, and explain the antibody finding is non-urgent — without dismissing or over-escalating.
